text:
Once upon a time, in a small valley full of old oak trees, lived a friendly mouse named Oliver.

Every morning, Oliver would hop out of his hole to enjoy the sunrise. One day, as he looked across the valley, he spotted a bright red balloon drifting lazily.

Oliver chased after it. He jumped, flew a little, and then fluttered down with it!

A beautiful songbird took notice. She admired the brave little mouse.

Together they soared high overhead. Everybody heard their song.

Years passed. The songbird grew old. She left heaven to visit old friends.

She didn’T return. Only the song lingered.

Now, every spring, folks would hear the song. They’d look up. They’d see that little owl perched proudly near the moon.

They named him Nightsong.

And that's how birds could sing.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
